sections.paragraph:
pl. גֹּורָלִים [this pl. not found] and גֹּורָלֹות m. prop. a little stone, pebble, κλῆρος, specially such as were used in casting lots.

(1) a lot, Lev 16:8, seq. Of casting lots, there are used the verbs יָרָה, יָדַד, הִשְׁלִיךְ, הִפִּיל, הֵטִיל, נָתַן, (which see); of a lot cast, נָפַל Jon 1:7 Eze 24:6 of a lot coming forth from the shaken urn, there is said, עָלָה גֹּורָל עַל Lev 16:9, and יָצָא גֹּורָל לְ Num 33:54 Jos 19:1, seq. A thing concerning which lots are cast is construed with עַל Psa 22:19 אֶל Joel 4:3 .

(2) what falls to any one by lot, especially part of an inheritance, land which falls to any one by inheritance; Jud 1:3, עֲלֵה אִתִּי בְּגֹורָלִי “come up with me into my lot,” my portion received by lot, Isa 57:6 Psa 125:3. Metaph. used of the lots assigned by God to men, Psa 16:5 Dan 12:13, וְתַעֲמֹד לְגֹרָֽלְךָ “and thou shalt rise that thou mayest share in thy lot at the end of the days,” in the kingdom of the Messiah (compare Rev 20:6).
